TRIO_DMA_DESC_t Union Reference
[TRIO Device API (gxio/trio.h)]

TRIO DMA Descriptor. More...


Data Fields

struct {
   int_reg_t   va: 42
 Tile side virtual address.
   uint_reg_t   bsz: 3
 Encoded size of buffer used on push DMA when C=1: 0 = 128 bytes 1 = 256 bytes 2 = 512 bytes 3 = 1024 bytes 4 = 1664 bytes 5 = 4096 bytes 6 = 10368 bytes 7 = 16384 bytes.
   uint_reg_t   c: 1
 Chaining designation.
   uint_reg_t   notif: 1
 Notification interrupt will be delivered when the transaction has completed (all data has been read from or written to the Tile-side buffer).
   uint_reg_t   smod: 1
 When 0, the XSIZE field specifies the total byte count for the transaction.
   uint_reg_t   xsize: 14
 Total number of bytes to move for this transaction.
   uint_reg_t   t: 1
 Descriptor type.

 Generation number.
   uint_reg_t   io_address: 64
 IO-side address.
}; 
uint_reg_t words [2]
 Word access.

Detailed Description

TRIO DMA Descriptor.

The TRIO DMA descriptor is written by software and consumed by hardware. It is used to specify the location of transaction data in the IO and Tile domains.

Examples:

trio/push_dma/app.c.

Field Documentation

int_reg_t TRIO_DMA_DESC_t::va

Tile side virtual address.

Examples:
trio/push_dma/app.c.

uint_reg_t TRIO_DMA_DESC_t::bsz

Encoded size of buffer used on push DMA when C=1: 0 = 128 bytes 1 = 256 bytes 2 = 512 bytes 3 = 1024 bytes 4 = 1664 bytes 5 = 4096 bytes 6 = 10368 bytes 7 = 16384 bytes.

uint_reg_t TRIO_DMA_DESC_t::c

Chaining designation.

Always zero for pull DMA 0 : Unchained buffer pointer 1 : Chained buffer pointer. Next buffer descriptor (e.g. VA) stored in 1st 8-bytes in buffer. For chained buffers, first 8-bytes of each buffer contain the next buffer descriptor formatted exactly like a mPIPE buffer descriptor. This allows a chained mPIPE buffer to be sent using push DMA.

uint_reg_t TRIO_DMA_DESC_t::notif

Notification interrupt will be delivered when the transaction has completed (all data has been read from or written to the Tile-side buffer).

uint_reg_t TRIO_DMA_DESC_t::smod

When 0, the XSIZE field specifies the total byte count for the transaction.

When 1, the XSIZE field is encoded as 2^(N+14) for N in {0..6}: 0 = 16 kB 1 = 32 kB 2 = 64 kB 3 = 128 kB 4 = 256 kB 5 = 512 kB 6 = 1 MB All other encodings of the XSIZE field are reserved when SMOD=1

uint_reg_t TRIO_DMA_DESC_t::xsize

Total number of bytes to move for this transaction.

When SMOD=1, this field is encoded - see SMOD description.

uint_reg_t TRIO_DMA_DESC_t::t

Descriptor type.

Must be zero.

uint_reg_t TRIO_DMA_DESC_t::gen

Generation number.

Used to indicate a valid descriptor in ring. When a new descriptor is written into the ring, software must toggle this bit. The net effect is that the GEN bit being written into new descriptors toggles each time the ring tail pointer wraps.

uint_reg_t TRIO_DMA_DESC_t::io_address

IO-side address.

uint_reg_t TRIO_DMA_DESC_t::words[2]

Word access.

The documentation for this union was generated from the following file:
  • arch/trio_shm.h
